title: Catalyst Watch: Earnings heat up, Fedspeak flurry, and eyes on space
content:
[Wall street and Broadway road sign in New York City]

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - APRIL 13

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Coherent (COHR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COHR]) and AMD (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Mega Fortune (MGRT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGRT]), Avis Budget (CAR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAR]), and Tilly’s (TLYS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TLYS]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include MDxHealth SA (MDXH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDXH]), PepGen (PEPG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEPG]), and Byrna Technologies (BYRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYRN]). 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Goldman Sachs (GS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GS]) and Fastenal (FAST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FAST]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Abbott Labs (ABT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABT]), AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), Johnson Outdoors (JOUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JOUT]), and Acuity Brands (AYI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AYI]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Encore Medical (EMI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EMI]), Madison Air Solutions (MAIR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAIR]), and Arxis (ARXS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARXS]) are expected to price their IPOs next week and begin to trade. The quiet period ends on Janus Living (JAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JAN]) and Guardian Metal Resources (GMTL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GMTL]) to free up analysts to post ratings. The countdown for investors to see SpaceX's (SPACE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPACE]) financials ahead of a June IPO continues. 
 	* ALL WEEK - Federal Reserve commentary ramps up next week with remarks from Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler on Monday; Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari, Boston Fed President Susan Collins, Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin, and Vice Chair for Supervision Michael Barr on Tuesday; Governor Michelle Bowman and Barr again on Wednesday; and New York Fed President John Williams on Thursday.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Space Symposium will begin. The broad space industry conference that brings together leaders from government, defense, commercial space, and research organizations. Northrop Grumman (NOC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NOC]) and Lockheed Martin (LMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LMT]) are two of the notable companies participating. The symposium has added significance after the Artemis mission and anticipated increase in defense-related spending.
 	* ALL DAY - OPEC will publish its Monthly Oil Market Report.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day CinemaCon event in Las Vegas begins. Studios with presentations scheduled include Sony Pictures (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]), Warner Bros. (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]), Universal Pictures (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]), Amazon MGM Studios (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), Paramount Pictures (PSKY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSKY]), and Walt Disney Studios (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]).

TUESDAY - APRIL 14

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include JPMorgan Chase (JPM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JPM]), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ]), Wells Fargo (WFC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WFC]), Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), BlackRock (BLK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLK]), BetMGM (MGM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGM]), and Albertsons (ACI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACI]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for CarMax (KMX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KMX]) after it reports.
 	* ALL DAY - The March Producer Price Index report will be released. Traders will be watching for any reactions from the oil price shock.
 	* ALL DAY - The IMF will issue its World Outlook report.

WEDNESDAY - APRIL 15

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include ASML (ASML [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASML]), Bank of America (BAC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAC]), Morgan Stanley (MS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MS]), J.B. Hunt (JBHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBHT]), and PNC Financial (PNC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PNC]).
 	* ALL DAY - Needham will hold its virtual healthcare conference featuring more than two dozen companies.
 	* ALL DAY - The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts. 

THURSDAY - APRIL 16

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Taiwan Semi (TSM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSM]), PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]), Charles Schwab (SCHW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SCHW]), Abbott Lab (ABT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABT]), USB (USB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/USB]), and Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]).
 	* ALL DAY - Carnival (CCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CCL]) shareholders will vote on board appointments, say-on-pay authorization, and proposals connected to unifying the current dual‑listed structure into a single NYSE‑listed entity and related redomiciliation to Bermuda.
 	* ALL DAY - The Philadelphia Fed Index report will be released. Economists will be watching to see if the index stays in expansionary territory.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with SunOpta (STKL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STKL]) will vote on the buyout offer from Refresco.
 	* 8:15 A.M. PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]) will hold its earnings conference call. Investors will be watching for color on the North America snacks business after management flagged plans to cut some snack prices and simplify the U.S. assortment under pressure from activist Elliott Investment Management. Options trading implies a share price move of 6% after the report is released. Celsius Holdings (CELH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CELH]) and McCormick (MKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MKC]) are two suppliers/partners that have a strong share price correlation to PepsiCo on earnings day.

FRIDAY - APRIL 17

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Ally Financial (ALLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALLY]) and Regions Financial (RF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RF]).
 	* ALL DAY - Boeing (BA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BA]) will hold its annual meeting. The board has recommended that shareholders vote against a proposal to allow action by written consent by shareholders.
 	* ALL DAY - The NAB Show annual trade show will begin. The event produced by the National Association of Broadcasters brings together broadcasters, streamers, studios, tech vendors, and creators across TV, radio, film, streaming, and digital media. Notable participating companies on the show floor or program include Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]), Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]), Dell Technologies (DELL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DELL]), and Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]). Speakers from Disney (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]), NBCUniversal (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]), YouTube (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]), and TikTok (TIKTOK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TIKTOK]) are expected.
 	* ALL DAY - The AACR Annual Meeting in San Diego will begin. The event is a high‑signal oncology catalyst, with big pharma and emerging biotechs set to showcase new data and pipeline updates across targeted therapies, immuno‑oncology, ADCs, and next‑generation platforms. Analysts have circled Bayer, Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), Pfizer (PFE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PFE]), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JNJ]), Merck (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]), and Genentech/Roche.
 	* ALL DAY - The Federal Reserve blackout period ahead of its next meeting begins.
 	* All day - The tender offer for Neurocrine Biosciences (NBIX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NBIX]) to acquire Soleno Therapeutics (SLNO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SLNO]) expires.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

MORE ON THE MARKETS

 	* March CPI: Inflation Pumps Up As Gasoline Soars [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4889816-march-cpi-inflation-pumps-up-as-gasoline-soars]
 	* Commodities to lead 'anything but bonds' trade through 2020s - BofA’s Hartnett [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4574044-commodities-to-lead-anything-but-bonds-trade-through-2020s---bofas-hartnett]
 	* U.S., EU move closer to critical minerals pact to counter Beijing's dominance - report [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4573990-us-eu-move-closer-to-critical-minerals-pact-to-counter-beijings-dominance---report]

title: Kingdom Capital initiates AENT; exits multiple positions in Q1 2026
content:
* Kingdom Capital Advisors delivered an approximately 8.0% net return in Q1 2026, significantly outperforming the Russell 2000 Total Return’s 0.89% gain and the S&P 500’s 4.33% decline.
 	* The portfolio took on a new position in Alliance Entertainment (NASDAQ: AENT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AENT]) during Q1 2026. 
 	* In Q1 2026, the fund exited all positions in TSS (NASDAQ: TSSI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSSI]), Energous (NASDAQ: WATT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WATT]), SunOpta (NASDAQ: STKL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STKL]), Ziff Davis (NASDAQ: ZD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ZD]), and Eastman Kodak (NYSE: KODK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KODK]).
 	* Source [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4888805-kingdom-capital-advisors-q1-2026-investor-letter].

title: Kingdom Capital Advisors Generated About 50% Return in One Month from Its Investment in SunOpta (STKL)
content:
Kingdom Capital Advisors, a registered investment advisor, released its first quarter 2026 investor letter. A copy of the letter is available to download here. The first quarter of 2026 delivered a strong performance, despite the market volatility driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The portfolio thrived by avoiding the significant downturn in AI-driven software stocks and benefiting from several expected catalysts in special situation investments. Kingdom Capital Advisors (KCA Value Composite) returned 8.01% (after fees) in the first quarter, outperforming the Russell 2000 TR at 0.89%, the S&P 500 TR at -4.33%, and the NASDAQ 100 TR at -5.82The composite compounded at 22.81% net annualized versus 4.80% for the Russell 2000, since its inception in January 2022, marking cumulative outperformance of over 115%. The Firm continues to maintain a balanced portfolio of special situation and deep value investments, positioning the composite to deliver strong returns in the future. In addition, please check the Composite’s top five holdings to know its best picks in 2026.

In its first-quarter 2026 investor letter, Kingdom Capital Advisors highlighted SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL). SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) is a multinational food company that engages in the manufacture and sale of plant and fruit-based food and beverage products. On April 7, 2026, SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) closed at $6.48 per share. One-month return of SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) was 0.31%, and its shares gained 60.00% over the past 52 weeks. SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) has a market capitalization of $768.6 million.

Kingdom Capital Advisors stated the following regarding SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) in its Q1 2026 investor letter:

"SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL): After meeting management at the ICR Conference, we developed conviction in the company’s competitive positioning in shelf-stable plant-based milk and fruit snacks. We began building a position with the expectation it could become a core holding. However, the company agreed to be acquired for $6.50 per share shortly thereafter. While we would have preferred a larger position, the investment generated a return of nearly 50% in less than one month."Is SunOpta, Inc. (STKL) the Best Stock to Buy According to Howard Marks’ Oaktree Capital Management?

SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) is not on our list of 40 Most Popular Stocks Among Hedge Funds Heading Into 2026. According to our database, 25 hedge fund portfolios held SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) at the end of the fourth quarter, compared to 30 in the previous quarter. While we acknowledge the potential of SunOpta Inc. (NASDAQ:STKL) as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

Story Continues

In addition, please check out our hedge fund investor letters Q4 2025 page for more investor letters from hedge funds and other leading investors.

Disclosure: None. This article is originally published at Insider Monkey.

title: 1 High-Flying Stock with Exciting Potential and 2 Facing Challenges
content:
"You get what you pay for" often applies to expensive stocks with best-in-class business models and execution. While their quality can sometimes justify the premium, they typically experience elevated volatility during market downturns when expectations change.

Finding the right balance between price and quality can challenge even the most skilled investors. Luckily for you, we started StockStory to help you identify the real opportunities. That said, here is one high-flying stock expanding its competitive advantage and two climbing an uphill battle.

Two High-Flying Stocks to Sell:

OneWater (ONEW)

Forward P/E Ratio: 50.3x

A public company since early 2020, OneWater Marine (NASDAQ:ONEW) sells boats, yachts, and other marine products.

Why Do We Steer Clear of ONEW?

Poor same-store sales performance over the past two years indicates it’s having trouble bringing new shoppers into its brick-and-mortar locations Performance over the past three years shows its incremental sales were much less profitable, as its earnings per share fell by 53.6% annually High net-debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 7× could force the company to raise capital at unfavorable terms if market conditions deteriorate

At $9.92 per share, OneWater trades at 50.3x forward P/E. Check out our free in-depth research report to learn more about why ONEW doesn’t pass our bar.

SunOpta (STKL)

Forward P/E Ratio: 36.6x

Committed to clean-label foods, SunOpta (NASDAQ:STKL) is a sustainability-focused food and beverage company specializing in the sourcing, processing, and packaging of organic products.

Why Are We Out on STKL?

Products have few die-hard fans as sales have declined by 1.6% annually over the last three years Revenue base of $792.4 million puts it at a disadvantage compared to larger competitors exhibiting economies of scale Easily substituted products (and therefore stiff competition) result in an inferior gross margin of 15.5% that must be offset through higher volumes

SunOpta’s stock price of $6.49 implies a valuation ratio of 36.6x forward P/E. Read our free research report to see why you should think twice about including STKL in your portfolio, it’s free.

One High-Flying Stock to Buy:

Core Natural Resources (CNR)

Forward P/E Ratio: 45.9x

Tracing its origins to 1864 and operating some mines southwest of Pittsburgh, Core Natural Resources (NYSE:CNR) mines and exports metallurgical coal used in steelmaking and thermal coal for power generation.

Why Is CNR a Good Business?

Annual revenue growth of 15.1% over the past nine years was outstanding, reflecting market share gains this cycle Economies of scale give it some operating leverage when demand rises Strong free cash flow margin of 13.6% enables it to reinvest or return capital consistently

Story Continues

Core Natural Resources is trading at $102.50 per share, or 45.9x forward P/E. Is now a good time to buy? Find out in our full research report, it’s free.

Stocks We Like Even More

ONE MORE THING: Top 5 Growth Stocks. The biggest stock winners almost always had one thing in common before they ran. Revenue growing like crazy. Meta. CrowdStrike. Broadcom. Our AI flagged all three. They returned 315%, 314%, and 455%, respectively.

Find out which 5 stocks it's flagging for this month — FREE.  Get Our Top 5 Growth Stocks for Free HERE.

Stocks that made our list in 2020 include now familiar names such as Nvidia (+1,326% between June 2020 and June 2025) as well as under-the-radar businesses like the once-small-cap company Comfort Systems (+782% five-year return). Find your next big winner with StockStory today.
